title: Malvastrum niveum
intro: Malvastrum niveum is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, commonly known as the hollyhock or mallow family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Malvastrum niveum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Malvastrum niveum is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Malvastrum niveum is a member of the Malvaceae family, which includes hibiscus, hollyhock, and okra. It belongs to the genus Malvastrum, which is native to Africa. The plant is often classified under the Malvales order and the Asterids clade. Malvastrum niveum is also sometimes referred to as a member of the Sphaeralcea family.
